title: Falmouth Harbour in UK Uses Renewable Diesel for Workboat Fleet
content: Falmouth Harbour announced in August that it is now among the harbors and port authorities in the U.K.Â using hydrotreated vegetable oil (HVO), also known as renewable diesel,Â to fuel its fleet of workboats.Â A Â£50,000 (USD$67,775), 6,500-liter (1,717-gallon) refueling facility has been created at the harborâ€™s marine hub with 50 percent match-funding from Cornwall Councilâ€™s Growth Hub Grants Programme. The HVO fuel it provides is produced from renewable sources like used cooking oil and vegetable oils that absorb CO2 during plant growth.
author: Analysts Account
